sourceSummary: MIT Open Learning resources that can inform K-12 curriculum design, competency-based progression, and LUMI content generation.
sourceNotes: The MIT Learn and Open Learning pages referenced in this catalog are public-facing resource hubs rather than a single downloadable dataset.
The content below captures reusable metadata patterns for curriculum planning and LUMI prompts rather than full copyrighted course content.
curriculumDesignPrinciples: Competency-based progression with transparent learning targets
Modular lesson design that can be sequenced into units or micro-lessons
Formative assessment through problem sets, projects, and reflection prompts
Inquiry-based and hands-on learning for STEM and interdisciplinary topics
Flexible enrichment pathways for students above grade level

resources.name: MIT OpenCourseWare Educator Portal
lumiUse: generate lesson scaffolds from course modules

resources.name: MITx on edX
curriculumUse: borrow course structure for high-school pathways
adapt lecture sequencing into short modules
use quizzes and projects as assessment templates
lumiUse: generate study plans by skill level

resources.name: MIT pK-12 and Playful Learning
curriculumUse: connect projects to standards and competencies

recommendedLumiApplications: Create lesson plans that combine competency targets with MIT-style modular content.
Generate K-12 curriculum trees with prerequisite sequencing and enrichment branches.
Recommend formative assessments, projects, and reflection tasks based on MIT-inspired pedagogy.
Surface teacher-facing resources for classroom implementation and differentiation.
